message:
fix(db): route file-backed ATC experience IO through canonical SQLite; Clippy/style pass across all crates

Two primary themes: (1) work around a FrankenConnection page-ordering
bug that corrupts ATC experience rows in file-backed mailboxes by
routing those writes through canonical C SQLite, and (2) a broad
Clippy/style improvement pass across CLI, DB, server, and tools crates.

## ATC experience canonical SQLite routing (queries.rs)

ATC raw experience rows are the only runtime table family still observed
corrupting file-backed mailboxes under FrankenConnection writes. Until
the upstream page-ordering/runtime bug is fixed, all file-backed ATC
experience IO now goes through CanonicalDbConn (C SQLite) via a
parallel synchronous implementation.

New canonical ATC infrastructure:
- open_canonical_atc_conn(): opens a CanonicalDbConn with PRAGMA
  conn settings for ATC operations
- begin/commit/rollback_canonical_atc_write_tx(): transaction lifecycle
  for canonical ATC writes using BEGIN IMMEDIATE
- canonical_query_atc_rows(), canonical_execute_atc(): query/execute
  helpers with contextual error messages
- canonical_fetch_atc_experience_by_decision_effect(),
  canonical_fetch_atc_experience_by_id(): canonical read helpers

New file-backed operation implementations:
- append_atc_experience_file_backed(): INSERT via canonical SQLite with
  ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, post-commit re-select verification
- transition_atc_experience_file_backed(): SELECT-UPDATE cycle via
  canonical SQLite with state machine validation, timestamp progression,
  non-execution reason handling, and context patch merging
- fetch_atc_rollups_file_backed(): rollup aggregation via canonical read

Routing: fetch_durable_atc_experience_by_decision_effect() and
fetch_durable_atc_experience_by_id() now prefer canonical reads for
file-backed pools. ensure_file_backed_atc_pool_initialized() verifies
atc_experiences table exists before first use.

New type: AtcRollupRow pub type alias for rollup tuple.

New test: canonical_atc_writes_pass_quick_check verifies file-backed
ATC writes through the canonical path preserve SQLite integrity via
PRAGMA quick_check.

## Schema user_version synchronization (schema.rs, cli/lib.rs, reconstruct.rs)

New schema_user_version_sql() helper returns the PRAGMA user_version
statement for the current schema version. Applied after migration
(handle_migrate_with_database_url_locked) and after archive
reconstruction (reconstruct_from_archive) to keep the SQLite
user_version field aligned with the application schema version.

## Doctor improvements (cli/lib.rs)

- FTS5 check inverted: now checks for legacy SQLite FTS5 tables
  (presence is a warning since Search V3/Tantivy is authoritative;
  absence is the expected healthy state)
- guard_hooks check gracefully handles missing git repo (returns "ok"
  with skip message instead of "warn")
- New doctor_legacy_fts_tables() helper scans sqlite_master for legacy
  FTS5 virtual tables by name pattern and USING clause
- Archive git repo discovery (discover_archive_git_repos) now also
  checks the storage root itself for a .git directory

## Pool deferred write queue improvements (pool.rs)

Lock scope reduction: now_us and entry_bytes are computed before
acquiring the queue mutex in enqueue(); lock is dropped before
returning the DeferralOutcome. drain() and pressure() also drop
the lock before processing results. This reduces contention on the
deferred write queue under high-concurrency workloads.

Also: RecoveryAdmissionController gains Default impl, #[must_use],
PoisonError import, Duration::from_mins(), and various doc formatting.

## Reconstruct agent_link merge refactoring (reconstruct.rs)

Paired source/target ID lookups for agent_link rows now use zip()
instead of 4 separate let-Some guards, reducing nesting depth and
improving readability without changing semantics.

## Clippy/style improvements across all crates

Broad Clippy-driven improvements applied consistently across CLI, DB,
server (including all 14 TUI screens), and tools crates:

- if-let chains: collapsed nested if-let-else patterns into combined
  guard patterns (agents, analytics, archive_browser, atc, attachments,
  contacts, dashboard, explorer, messages, projects, reservations,
  search, threads, timeline, tool_metrics, tui_web_dashboard)
- map_or_else: replaced if-let-Some-else with map_or_else in
  identity.rs, startup_checks.rs, and similar patterns
- const fn: added to pure accessors and zero-side-effect methods
- Self type: replaced explicit type names with Self in const arrays
- Lifetime elision: removed unnecessary explicit lifetime parameters
- #[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)]: applied where appropriate
- Duration::from_mins/from_secs constants: replaced manual calculations
- PoisonError::into_inner: imported directly instead of closure
- is_some_and: replaced map_or(false, ...) patterns
- Various minor: close_db_conn instead of bare drop, map_err(Box::new)
  for consistent error boxing, to_string_lossy patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
